title: Evergrande Founder Says Sorry for Money Problems
summary: Hui Ka Yan, who started the big Chinese company Evergrande, said he was guilty in court. He did wrong things like taking money that was meant for building houses and using it for other projects. This happened in Shenzhen, China. Evergrande was once a huge real estate company worth over $50 billion, but it got into big trouble in 2021 because it owed too much money. The company had many projects across China that were not finished. Hui used to be very rich, but now he is in trouble. The court will decide his punishment later. This case is important because Evergrande's collapse hurt China's property market and made investors and banks lose money.

cash or funds that people use to buy things or build projects

Where did the court hearing happen?
Why did Evergrande get into trouble?
What will the court do next?
options: Give Hui Ka Yan a prize

background_read: Evergrande was a big company in China that built houses. It started in 1996 and grew very fast because China's economy was booming. Many people bought houses from Evergrande before they were built, but the company used that money for other things instead of finishing the houses. This caused problems when Evergrande could not pay back all the money it borrowed. In 2021, it collapsed, which means it stopped working properly. This hurt many people who invested in it and banks that lent it money. Hui Ka Yan, the founder, used to be one of the richest people in Asia, but now he is facing legal trouble for his actions.
Article_Structure: WHO: Hui Ka Yan, the founder of Evergrande
WHAT: He pleaded guilty to taking money meant for building houses and using it for other projects
WHERE: In a court in Shenzhen, China
WHY: Because Evergrande collapsed due to too much debt, hurting China's property market and investors

perspectives.description: Hui Ka Yan is facing justice for breaking the law by misusing funds.
Evergrande's collapse shows the risks of too much borrowing in China's property market.
